sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Julia Jones, a 25-year-old female, presents for evaluation of breast reduction.
She has been experiencing symptoms of macromastia, including back pain, neck pain, and bra strap indentations.
She reports that these symptoms have been present since high school.
She has tried nonsurgical treatments with minimal relief.
She is considering breast reduction surgery to alleviate these symptoms.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of breast cancer in her family, with her mother being diagnosed at 40 years old.
She has a family history of breast cancer.
She has been going to the chiropractor for back pain and has been taking propranolol for migraines.
Review of systems: The patient reports numbness and tingling in her hands and nipple pain.
She denies rashes or nipple discharge.
Current medications: Propranolol.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: Physical exam findings of breast, inspection reveals asymmetrical breasts with severe ptosis bilaterally.
There is no nipple retraction or discharge bilaterally.
No breast tenderness, masses, or axillary lymphadenopathy is palpable.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: Julia Jones, a 25-year-old female with symptomatic macromastia, is an ideal candidate for bilateral reduction mammoplasty.
Problem list: Macromastia; Status: active; Breast cancer family history; Status: past; Numbness and tingling in hands; Status: active; Nipple pain; Status: active.
Plan:
I prescribed no medications at this time.
I will document the patient's symptoms and measurements, and submit them to the insurance company for approval.
Once the patient is on the new insurance plan, we will resubmit for approval.
I will take photos of the patient's breasts for documentation purposes.
I will meet with the patient again before the surgery to answer any last-minute questions and review instructions in more detail.
The patient will need to be on no heavy lifting for 6 weeks post-operatively, and no driving for 2-3 weeks post-operatively.
Follow-up:
The patient will need to return for a pre-operative consultation once the insurance has approved the procedure.
